rate_note: rate_hz is the requested IMU target; iPhone fused deviceMotion caps ~100 Hz regardless. Always derive the true rate from host_ts. Aiding channels run at their own rates.

clock.rtcp_sync: unix_ts matches RTP RTCP SR NTP timeline

pose_units: type9: ARKit world pose — translation xyz f32 meters (@24), trackingState f32 (@36), gravityTilt f32 deg (@40), gravityAzimuth f32 deg (@44), quaternion xyzw f32 (@48). Frame: gravity-aligned world (+Y up, yaw arbitrary), origin at session start. flags(@1): bit0 discontinuity (re-anchor here), bit1 relocalized, bit2 kinematic jump (silent loop closure), bit3 operator reset tracking (BRAND-NEW world frame: new origin AND new yaw — nothing before it is expressible in the new frame; start a fresh epoch, do not carry any transform across). host_ts stays continuous across a reset (same uptime axis as the video PTS).
pose_gravity: gravityTilt = degrees between ARKit world +Y and TRUE gravity (CoreMotion). ARKit learns gravity from motion, so a near-stationary session can run tens of degrees off vertical with trackingState=normal. NON-ZERO TILT MEANS THE WORLD FRAME IS NOT LEVEL — walk the phone around to let it converge. gravityAzimuth = atan2(z,x) of world-frame gravity's horizontal component (unstable as tilt->0). Rebuild world gravity as g=(sin t cos a, -cos t, sin t sin a). NaN in raw IMU mode (no fused gravity).
gnss_units: type6: lat,lon f64 degrees; altitude,hAcc,vAcc f32 m; speed f32 m/s; course f32 deg; speedAcc f32 m/s (negatives = invalid)
altitude_units: type7: relativeAltitude f32 m (vs start), pressure f32 kPa
